references.title: Early-predicting dropout of university students: an application of innovative multilevel machine learning and statistical techniques

extracted.Data sources: Student Demographics, Previous Academic Performance, Academic Information
extracted.Features: [Gender, Income (range), Access to Study Age, Student’ origins, Previous Studies, Admission score, TotalCredits1s, Attempts 1s]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Data is collectedbetween 2010/2011 and 2015/2016.

Dataset is subsequently divided between early and late dropout and using different configurations:

- Degree courses not considered

- Courses considered as dummies

- Courses considered as part of a multilevel model

In the last formulation, a random effects increment is added to the intercept to account for the probability of a course contributing to dropout.
extracted.Models: Generalized Linear Model, Decision Tree, Random Forest
extracted.Comments:
Authors use 3 different configurations of models to perform 2 distinct classification tasks:

Late Dropout vs Graduated,

Eearly Dropout vs Graduated

From results, it becomesapparent that detecting early dropout is relatively easier since most early dropouts are justified either by poor performance from the get go - which may indicate some difficulty to adapt.
extracted.Limitations:
- Holdout Method

- No integration of Course Interaction Data

- Only consider binary classification scenarios

- Small number of algorithms considered

annotations.text: Combines theoretically based model with data driven approach to create early warning system fro Dropout.
Model uses multi level statistics and results show effectiveness of approach.
